 {"id":"ft-5hp7p","title":"[config-types][test] extracted crate tests miss toml dev-dependency","description":"frankenterm-core-config-types inline tests use toml::to_string_pretty/from_str in tuning_config.rs, but the extracted crate manifest only declared serde. cargo test -p frankenterm-core-config-types failed with E0433 unresolved crate toml until the dev-dependency is restored.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:05.296081Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:40.437023Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:40.436733Z","close_reason":"Added the missing toml dev-dependency so cargo test -p frankenterm-core-config-types passes. Landed in 08e48279.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5m5xc","title":"scan_pipeline: enable_ansi_analysis config field is dead — never read, setting false has zero effect","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62 ScanPipelineConfig declares 'pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool' with Default = true, but no code in the crate ever reads this field. process() at :405 and process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally call scan_newlines_and_ansi* regardless of the flag. An operator configuring 'enable_ansi_analysis = false' in their config expecting to disable the ANSI byte counting gets no behavior change. This is a reality-gap defect: the documented config surface lies about what it controls. Fix: gate the ansi-scan call on self.config.enable_ansi_analysis; when false, skip the scan and return a ScanMetricsSummary with ansi_byte_count=0 and ansi_density=0 (preserving the newline_count + logical_lines numbers that don't depend on ANSI scanning). Default stays true so existing callers see no change. Regression test: set enable_ansi_analysis=false, feed input containing ESC bytes, assert ansi_byte_count==0 in returned ScanOutput.metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:35.328044Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053447Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3a4dfcd8 (fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths). Zero ansi_byte_count after SIMD scan when flag is false; scan still runs to keep state.scan_state honest across chunks. Two regression tests pin batch (process) + chunked (process_chunk) contracts, including a sanity check that the default config still counts ANSI bytes. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5n9my","title":"storage reopen preserves synchronous FTS indexing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:57.132270Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.698143Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.697874Z","close_reason":"Landed in commit 49937d56: storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing test pins the full StorageHandle reopen path. Asserts (1) fresh open with defer_fts_triggers=false installs all 3 output_segments_a[iud] triggers and synchronously indexes the first write, (2) reopen with defer_fts_triggers=false keeps all 3 triggers and synchronously indexes the second write via the trigger path. Combined with existing fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle (SQL-level true→false phase test) and fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip (5-iteration stress), the ft-ih4tm bidirectional contract is now pinned at both the connection and StorageHandle layers. Added Debug+Clone on StorageConfig for test ergonomics. All 3 tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-5qph8","title":"[gui][i18n][test] ft-6scm7 follow-on: render-level golden test missing for RTL+Indic shaping","description":"Closing commit for ft-6scm7 (ab11211d) shipped paragraph-context plumbing through cached_cluster_shape -> font.shape, with byte_offset translation in PresentationWidth so harfbuzz cluster indices map back to per-cluster cell indices. Net behavior fix is sound.

Acceptance gap: ft-6scm7's stated acceptance asked for 'companion render-golden test for at least one RTL + one Indic sample' (RTL string e.g. 'السلام عليكم' and Indic string e.g. 'नमस्ते'). The merged commit added cluster_paragraph_context_tracks_rtl_and_indic_ranges in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/screen_line.rs (tests module), which only exercises the new ClusterParagraphContext helper -- it asserts the helper concatenates cluster.text and tracks per-cluster Range<usize> offsets correctly. It does NOT exercise:
  - cached_cluster_shape paragraph_byte_offset path (the actual shaping integration)
  - cluster index rebasing in the Ok(mut info) branch (lines 850-863 of render/mod.rs)
  - PresentationWidth::with_cluster_and_byte_offset round-trip across harfbuzz output
  - Render-level pixel/glyph correctness for RTL embedding levels or Indic conjunct formation

Why P3 not P2: the unit-level helper test plus the existing shaper coverage downstream provides enough scaffolding that any regression would likely surface in shaper integration tests; visible mis-shaping would also surface in manual QA. But the bead's explicit acceptance criterion is unmet, so re-opening would be cleaner if we want strict hygiene. P3 is the right tier for 'acceptance gap, not a regression.'

Suggested fix: add an integration-style test (does not have to be GPU/golden -- can use the existing software shaping path) that:
  1. Builds a Line with mixed-direction text ('abc السلام مرحبا') and an Indic line ('hello नमस्ते world').
  2. Calls the same code path screen_line.rs uses to build cell_clusters + paragraph_context.
  3. Calls cached_cluster_shape via a TermWindow stub or extracts the shaping into a testable helper.
  4. Asserts the returned ShapedInfo cluster indices are within each cluster's local byte range (would have caught the absolute_cluster < offset error path).

Citation: crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/mod.rs:850-863 (rebase logic), crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/render/screen_line.rs:929-952 (existing test), shipped in commit ab11211d (ft-6scm7).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:01:03.044512Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:01:06.559510Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","i18n","review","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-5qph8","depends_on_id":"ft-6scm7","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:01:06.558976Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

All pass via rch. Closes acceptance gap from ft-6scm7. Commit 357fb60c.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gui","i18n","review","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-5qph8","depends_on_id":"ft-6scm7","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:01:06.558976Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-5qsbp","title":"[storage] Existing v0 DB init marks unrepaired old tables current","description":"Fresh-eyes review of the recent storage migration idempotency refactor found that initialize_schema's current==0 && !needs_init branch runs SCHEMA_SQL and immediately sets user_version to SCHEMA_VERSION. SCHEMA_SQL uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, so any old table that already exists is not altered. The branch only special-cases audit_actions.correlation_id, leaving other current columns/tables on existing old tables unrepaired while marking the DB current. Root cause: v0 partial DB handling treats CREATE IF NOT EXISTS as a schema repair mechanism. Fix by running the migration plan after SCHEMA_SQL so per-version idempotent helpers repair existing tables before user_version is marked current; add regression assertions to migration_runner_simulated_upgrade.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:41:40.693495Z","created_by":"RoseCat","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:23.826902Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:23.826438Z","close_reason":"Fresh-eyes follow-up fixed sparse v0 DB handling: pre-SCHEMA_SQL repairs now run only when audit_actions/workflow_step_logs/events already exist, so missing tables are created by the normal schema/migration path. Added migration_runner_sparse_v0_database_initializes. Verified rustfmt --edition 2024, git diff --check, remote rch cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib migration_runner_ (2 passed), and UBS changed-file scan with only pre-existing storage.rs inventory remaining.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","migration","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3636,"issue_id":"ft-5qsbp","author":"RoseCat","text":"Reopened: Fresh-eyes follow-up found a sparse-v0 miss in the previous repair: pre-repair helpers that create indexes must only run when their base table already exists.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:11:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-5v7l0","title":"[golden] wa.search conformance golden freezes float fusion weights","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_search.json currently freezes raw float bytes for metrics.lexical_weight=0.30000001192092896 and metrics.semantic_weight=0.699999988079071. Those values originate from effective_search_fusion_weights in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:150-157 via f32 casting of quality_weight.\n\nRisk: these golden bytes are tied to representation details of the current float path rather than operator-visible wa.search behavior. A harmless implementation change (for example widening/narrowing intermediates, different serde float rendering, or moving the helper) can cause golden churn with no semantic regression. This is the same class of byte-stability issue as ft-55l4f: scheduler/config representation detail frozen as contract noise.\n\nFix direction: keep a semantic assertion on the search weights in the test harness, but canonicalize or round the serialized golden value so the golden freezes presence/meaning rather than exact float formatting artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:11:54.151063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461737Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:25:21.461447Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 691661d2 (Option a: round to 2 decimals, matching pane 5 style precedent). canonicalize() in mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs now matches 'lexical_weight' | 'semantic_weight' if the child is a number, computes (f*100.0).round()/100.0, and stores it via serde_json::Number::from_f64 → Value::Number. The golden value is exactly 0.3 / 0.7 after round-trip because ryu emits shortest round-trip form for f64 0.3 / 0.7. wa_search.json golden pinned to the canonical 0.3 / 0.7 values. Kept the 30/70 split readable in the golden (reviewer can still see intent at a glance) instead of zeroing like score/semantic_score. Semantic assertions in assert_search_success_data at :446 already skip these fields so no test-logic change.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5zyqe","title":"[mock] dataflow.rs:980 merged compute nodes downgraded to NodeKind::Source","description":"dataflow.rs:980 in DataflowGraph::merge() sets `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder` for merged compute/debounce nodes because ComputeFn is not Clone. This silently degrades merged compute nodes to inert Source nodes — their computation logic is lost. Production behavior should be: either (a) add a NodeKind::InertCompute variant so callers can detect and re-register the compute fn, (b) make ComputeFn clonable via Arc wrapper, or (c) log a warning when a compute node is downgraded during merge. The doc comment at line 965-968 acknowledges the limitation and tells callers to re-register, but the silent downgrade is error-prone.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:39.280862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.126666Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50.062995Z","close_reason":"implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3599,"issue_id":"ft-5zyqe","author":"jemanuel","text":"Commit cef1c892 switches dataflow ComputeFn to Arc so merge() can preserve Compute/Debounce node kinds instead of downgrading them to Source. Updated merge docs, rewired merge() to clone node kinds, and added regression tests proving merged compute nodes still recompute and merged debounce nodes still buffer+flush. Local verification: rustfmt + git diff --check passed; UBS found no critical issues in crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs. Requested rch run is still spending the window in full-workspace sync with no compile/test result line yet.","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:39:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.8.3","title":"Sync push/pull for config + binary (explicit, non-destructive)","description":"# Task: Sync push/pull for config + binary (feature sync)

## Goal
Implement the core sync operations for:
- `~/.config/wa/**` (config)
- optional `wa` binary (explicit opt-in)

The intent is to make it easy to keep multiple machines consistent **without ever surprising the user**.

## Design principles
- **Explicitness over magic**: no background sync; only `push`/`pull`.
- **Plan-first**: always compute and display a plan; require `--apply` for real changes.
- **No silent overwrites**: overwriting requires confirmation and clear diff metadata.
- **Secret-aware**: never sync forbidden paths; never print secret contents.

## Path selection rules
- Config root is the canonical wa config directory.
- Include only known-safe config artifacts.
- Exclude patterns like:
  - `**/.env*`
  - `**/*token*`
  - `**/*secret*`
  - keychains/SSH keys

(Exact allow/deny rules are authored in the sync spec bead; this bead implements them.)

## Conflict semantics
When both sides changed:
- classify as `Conflict` and refuse to apply automatically
- provide remediation:
  - show which file conflicted
  - suggest manual resolution steps
  - (future) offer an interactive merge/choose-side mode

## Binary sync
- Treat binary sync as a separate category:
  - opt-in in config
  - prefer syncing from a trusted build machine to others
- Validate binary integrity (hash/size) before replacing.

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.8.5` (sync tests):
  - offline push/pull planning and dry-run output stability
  - conflict classification (no silent overwrite)
  - secret/path deny rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa sync push/pull` computes a deterministic plan for config + binary categories.
- `--dry-run` never modifies local or remote files.
- Applying changes requires `--apply` and refuses to overwrite without explicit confirmation.
- Conflicts are detected and surfaced as `Conflict` items (no auto-overwrite).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.8.4","title":"Sync exported DB snapshots (immutable artifacts, versioned filenames)","description":"# Task: Sync exported DB snapshots (immutable artifacts)

## Goal
Make it easy to move **database snapshots** between machines for debugging or continuity, without ever touching the live DB file.

## Safety model
- Only sync snapshots created via `wa export` / `wa diag bundle` (explicit export paths).
- Never sync the live DB file or WAL/SHM.
- Treat snapshots as immutable artifacts:
  - versioned filenames
  - no in-place overwrite
  - optional compression (if/when supported by export)

## Naming & versioning
- Filenames should encode enough metadata to be self-describing:
  - wa version
  - created timestamp (UTC)
  - workspace key (hashed/short)
  - optional host

## Push/pull behavior
- `push` uploads snapshot(s) to the target under an artifacts directory.
- `pull` downloads snapshots and places them under a local artifacts directory.
- Default behavior is additive (no overwrites).

## UX expectations
- Plans should clearly distinguish:
  - “syncing snapshots” (safe)
  - “syncing live DB” (forbidden; should never appear)

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.8.5` (sync tests):
  - live DB paths are rejected
  - snapshot filenames are stable and never overwritten
  - push/pull round-trips via local-directory backend

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa sync push` can push an exported snapshot to a target without overwriting existing files.
- `wa sync pull` can pull the same snapshot back and preserves the filename.
- Attempts to sync live DB paths are rejected by the planner.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeFox","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:32:55.813247688Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:19:27.076526144Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T08:19:27.076395181Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.8.5","title":"Sync tests: config diffing + dry-run output stability (feature sync)","description":"# Task: Sync tests (feature sync)

## Goal
Make `wa sync` safe and boring by locking down:
- **path selection rules** (what may sync vs must never sync)
- **plan generation** (what would happen)
- **dry-run output stability** (humans + automation can trust it)

Sync is inherently risky: it touches configs and binaries, and can easily become a secret-exfiltration or accidental-overwrite footgun. Tests are the safety net.

## Scope
- Unit + integration tests that run **offline** and **deterministically**.
- No real SSH, no real remote machines, no real secrets.

## Test strategy
### 1) Pure unit tests (no filesystem required)
- **Path allow/deny rules**
  - allow: `~/.config/wa/**` (except explicit denylist)
  - allow (optional): `wa` binary (explicit opt-in)
  - deny: any known secret file patterns (tokens, keyrings, `.env`, etc.)
- **Target resolution**
  - parse configured sync targets
  - explicit “default target” selection rules
  - stable ordering (targets and files are sorted deterministically)
- **Planning semantics**
  - classify changes as `Add` / `Update` / `Skip` / `Conflict`
  - ensure “would overwrite” requires an explicit confirmation gate

### 2) Filesystem integration tests (temp dirs)
- Build a temp “local” tree and a temp “remote” tree (local-directory transport backend).
- Exercise:
  - `push --dry-run` produces a plan but changes nothing
  - `pull --dry-run` produces a plan but changes nothing
  - “same content” yields a minimal/no-op plan
  - “different content” yields Update items (but still requires confirmation in non-dry-run)

### 3) Golden tests for dry-run output stability
- Snapshot the **dry-run plan output** (both modes):
  - TTY-ish human format (no ANSI in tests; use the plain renderer)
  - machine JSON format (stable schema + stable ordering)
- The golden tests should assert:
  - stable ordering of files/targets
  - stable wording for safety warnings
  - redaction is applied to any values that might contain secrets

## Logging & debuggability requirements
- Tests should capture and assert a few critical log lines:
  - which target selected
  - how many items in plan
  - whether an overwrite would have occurred
- Log output must never include file contents or secret values.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --features sync` includes deterministic tests that cover:
  - path selection allow/deny rules (including a “secret-ish” fixture)
  - plan generation for push + pull (no-op, update, conflict)
  - dry-run output stability (goldens/snapshots)
- Tests are fully offline (no SSH/network), fast, and reproducible.
- A regression that would accidentally sync a forbidden path fails loudly.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add an explicit assertion that no files are modified in dry-run tests (e.g., hash trees before/after).
  - Include at least one fixture that resembles a secret and ensure it is denied and never appears in output.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadeFox","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:33:08.991829068Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:02:46.457132987Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T08:02:46.457004688Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.9","title":"[EPIC] Ship readiness: docs, CI checks, release workflow, versioning","description":"# [EPIC] Ship readiness (docs + CI + release)

## Mission
Make v0.1.0 shippable:
- clear docs
- reliable CI signals
- predictable version/build metadata

## Why
A tool that controls terminals needs high trust and clear operator UX.

## Definition of done
- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo test` pass in CI.
- README + quickstart lets another engineer install and run wa.
- Version/build metadata is visible (`wa --version`).

## Success Criteria
- CI runs fmt/clippy/tests/e2e reliably and preserves artifacts on failure.
- Release workflow/versioning produces reproducible binaries with build metadata.
- Docs/README/quickstart are sufficient for a new contributor to install and run wa.

## Testing
- CI verification:
  - CI must run the full quality gate (fmt/clippy/tests) and E2E, and upload artifacts on failure.

- Release smoke tests:
  - After packaging, run `wa --version` and `wa --help` and a minimal `wa doctor --format json` scenario to ensure the binary is functional.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

## Cross-references
- **wa-vv3h** (Rename project): Ship readiness depends on the final project name being settled. CI workflows, binary names, crate names, and documentation must all reflect the chosen name. Coordinate timing so that the rename lands before or as part of the v0.1.0 release to avoid publishing under a temporary name. README, quickstart, and `wa --version` output must use the canonical project name.

## CI: macOS + Linux Coverage
- CI matrix MUST include both macOS and Linux runners for the full quality gate:
  - `cargo fmt --check` (platform-independent, but verify).
  - `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` on both platforms to catch platform-conditional compilation warnings.
  - `cargo test` on both platforms to catch any `#[cfg(target_os)]` divergences.
  - E2E tests on Linux (Docker-native) and macOS (Docker Desktop) where Docker is required.
- Release binaries must be built for both `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` and `aarch64-apple-darwin` (Apple Silicon) at minimum, with `x86_64-apple-darwin` as a stretch target.
- Platform-specific smoke tests: verify `wa setup` handles launchd (macOS) vs systemd (Linux) correctly in CI for each platform.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:33:23.215105369Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:10:49.530535Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- **wa-vv3h** (Rename project): Ship readiness depends on the final project name being settled. CI workflows, binary names, crate names, and documentation must all reflect the chosen name. Coordinate timing so that the rename lands before or as part of the v0.1.0 release to avoid publishing under a temporary name. README, quickstart, and `wa --version` output must use the canonical project name.

## CI: macOS + Linux Coverage
- CI matrix MUST include both macOS and Linux runners for the full quality gate:
  - `cargo fmt --check` (platform-independent, but verify).
  - `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` on both platforms to catch platform-conditional compilation warnings.
  - `cargo test` on both platforms to catch any `#[cfg(target_os)]` divergences.
  - E2E tests on Linux (Docker-native) and macOS (Docker Desktop) where Docker is required.
- Release binaries must be built for both `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` and `aarch64-apple-darwin` (Apple Silicon) at minimum, with `x86_64-apple-darwin` as a stretch target.
- Platform-specific smoke tests: verify `wa setup` handles launchd (macOS) vs systemd (Linux) correctly in CI for each platform.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"codex-cobalt","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:33:23.215105369Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:02:36.626178Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.1","title":"CI pipeline: fmt/clippy/test/bench (feature matrix for mcp/web/tui/sync/browser)","description":"# Task: CI pipeline

## Goal
Ensure core quality checks run automatically on changes and failures are diagnosable from logs/artifacts.

## Core checks (blocking)
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`

## Feature matrix smoke (blocking or warn-only; decide explicitly)
Build/test the crate under common feature combinations so optional integrations don’t silently rot.

Baseline matrix (PLAN Appendix A/F driven):
- default features
- `--features mcp`
- `--features web`
- `--features tui`
- `--features sync`
- `--features browser`

Vendored lane (PLAN Appendix H):
- When `--features vendored` exists, CI must at least:
  - `cargo check --all-targets --features vendored`
  - and run a minimal smoke/unit subset that exercises vendored wiring

This is intentionally separated from the ROI decision (`wa-nu4.4.1.6`): once vendored is enabled, CI must keep it from rotting.

## Docs correctness lane
- Add a docs-smoke step/job that keeps README quickstart commands executable (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.9.9`).

## Related CI jobs (separate tasks)
- E2E job for real WezTerm scenarios: `wa-nu4.3.9.6`
- Setup idempotency job (safe dotfile patching in temp home): `wa-nu4.3.9.8`
- Vendored maintenance lane: `wa-nu4.4.1.7`

## Artifacts and logging
- On failure, upload:
  - test logs (stdout/stderr)
  - E2E artifacts (runner logs + captured outputs)
  - benchmark outputs when applicable

## Testing
- CI “tests the tests”:
  - Add a minimal job that prints the exact rustc/cargo versions and the exact feature-matrix commands being executed.
  - Ensure CI captures and uploads logs/artifacts for failing jobs so failures are actionable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI failures are actionable (clear logs, no flaky tests).
- Optional features (especially vendored) do not silently break over time.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.10","title":"Beads hygiene: eliminate bd lint template warnings (Acceptance Criteria headings, missing sections)","description":"# Task: Beads hygiene (template lint to zero)

## Goal
Make the Beads plan itself reliable and self-documenting by ensuring bd lint produces zero template warnings.

This improves:
- onboarding for future contributors
- clarity during implementation
- confidence that tasks have explicit acceptance criteria

## Scope
- For all open tasks/features/bugs:
  - ensure the description includes the required section heading(s):
    - tasks/features: ## Acceptance Criteria
    - bugs: ## Steps to Reproduce, ## Acceptance Criteria
  - ensure the acceptance criteria are concrete and testable

## Change discipline
- Do not delete or truncate existing content.
- Prefer minimal edits:
  - rename "## Acceptance" -> "## Acceptance Criteria"
  - add a missing section at the end if absent

## Acceptance Criteria
- bd lint --status open produces no warnings.
- New issues created in this project follow the template by default.


## Testing
- Run `bd lint` locally after any template edits.
- Add a simple CI check (if desired) that runs `bd lint --status open` and fails on warnings.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.9.11","title":"CI: shellcheck install.sh + scripts/install-hooks.sh (ship-gate for installer shell)","description":"## Gap

Ship-readiness epic wa-nu4.3.9 covers fmt/clippy/tests/release packaging, but the **public installer shell** (`install.sh` at repo root, the `curl | bash` one-liner target) and `scripts/install-hooks.sh` have zero CI coverage. A subtle bash bug there would break every fresh user install without CI catching it.

## Deliverable

Add a lightweight `installer-shell` job to `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that runs on Ubuntu and:

- `bash -n` syntax-checks `install.sh` and `scripts/install-hooks.sh`
- `shellcheck` (error+warning severity) against both scripts
- Fails the build on any finding

## Why not scripts/*.sh broadly

`scripts/*.sh` is a large internal surface with known style noise; a strict shellcheck pass there is a separate triage task. This bead is scoped to the two scripts that run on end-user machines outside our dev env, where a break = broken install day one.

## Acceptance

- New job visible in Actions tab
- Runs in < 60s (no Rust build)
- Both scripts parse clean under `bash -n`; both pass shellcheck at severity `warning`
- Part of the required ship-readiness gate (no `continue-on-error`)
